clinical_validity.rationale: The ED station remains clinically bounded and claim-limited while new evidence work avoids overstating simulation readiness.

psychometric_defensibility.rationale: The plan continues to separate trace-supported formative review from any high-stakes scoring claim.

technical_feasibility.rationale: Quest, local runtime, provider, GLB, audit, and manual performance gates now make feasibility blockers executable.

architecture_coherence.rationale: Benchmark, runtime, provider, asset, and architecture-rule evidence now align with the monorepo boundaries.

security_privacy.rationale: Local-only defaults, no cloud calls, no model downloads, audit gates, and license rejection behavior strengthen the security posture.

ux_workflow_fit.rationale: The XR shell, trace controls, manual Quest performance template, and admin handoff remain aligned to real user workflows.

cost_performance_efficiency.rationale: The plan avoids paid APIs and expensive downloads while still gathering useful local performance evidence.

open_source_sustainability.rationale: Pinned dependencies, license gating, and rejection of the LGPL transitive path improve sustainability.

market_gtm_strength.rationale: The demo path is more credible, but market evidence and design-partner validation remain future work.

evidence_discipline.rationale: The latest work turns key unknowns into commands, JSON reports, benchmark gates, and precise blockers.

implementation_readiness.rationale: The development team now has executable readiness probes for Quest, local runtime, provider mocks, GLB conversion, audit, and manual performance.

adversarial_robustness.rationale: The plan absorbed attacks on Quest theater, local AI halo effects, asset false readiness, and dependency optimism.

legal_regulatory_resilience.rationale: Claim limits, voice safety gates, dependency license notes, and no high-stakes assertions remain explicit.

specialty_clinical_generalizability.rationale: The architecture remains station-general, though clinical breadth still depends on later expert-reviewed case expansion.

critical_risks.summary: Stakeholders may confuse shell and interaction evidence with immersive Quest performance readiness.
Local AI and voice paths may be prematurely enabled before latency, safety, license, and thermal evidence exists.
Asset work may be underestimated because GLB conversion passed while Blender-backed generation and optimization remain unproven.

evidence_debt.summary: Capture and validate a foreground in-headset Quest manual performance report before claiming frame pacing or comfort readiness.
Install and benchmark one explicit local model runtime and model ID on target Apple Silicon hardware before enabling local dialogue.
Install and benchmark a local voice runtime only after safety and misuse review, then record first-audio latency and transcript/synthesis evidence.
Install Blender and run a small asset bake report before calling the 3D asset pipeline ready.

summary: Iteration 0008 improves maturity by converting Quest, local provider, asset, audit, and manual performance unknowns into executable evidence gates while preserving claim limits.
